channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: EALS Raises Concerns Over Potential Cross-Border Civic Restrictions in East Africa
item.description: The East Africa Law Society (EALS) has expressed growing concerns following remarks made during the Tanzania-Kenya Business Summit held in Dar es Salaam on May 4, 2026. The remarks, attributed to Tanz...

item.title: Bureau of Immigration arrests Vietnamese fugitive wanted by Interpol on drug charges at NAIA
item.description: The Bureau of Immigration (BI) successfully intercepted a 25-year-old Vietnamese woman upon her arrival at the Ninoy Aquino International Airport (NAIA) Terminal 1 last week. The woman, who came from ...

item.title: Enugu Police Arrest Man Posing as Military Officer and Recover Firearms
item.description: Police for Enugu State don arrest two people for separate wahala wey concern illegal possession of firearms and other dangerous items. One of the suspects, Victory Anyegbulam, 20, wey dey claim say na...

item.title: Khampepe Commission Continues Inquiry into Delays in TRC Case Prosecutions
item.description: The Khampepe Commission of Inquiry is currently sitting in Newtown, Johannesburg, to investigate attempts to obstruct the investigation and prosecution of apartheid-era perpetrators. The commission is...

item.title: 1950s Airline Stewardess Requirements Focused on Appearance Over Skill
item.description: In 1950s America, the hiring standards for stewardesses were focused entirely on appearance and personal image, rather than competence in aviation or safety. Airlines required women to be young, unmar...

item.title: Senior Portraits Reveal Teen Hairstyles and Fashion Trends in Early 1980s America
item.description: Senior portraits from Brookville High School’s Class of 1982 offer a vivid glimpse into teenage life and style during the early 1980s. These formal photographs, framed by simple studio lighting and pl...

item.title: The Visionary Fashion of Kansai Yamamoto in the 1970s
item.description: Kansai Yamamoto, born in Yokohama in 1944, was a pioneering Japanese fashion designer known for his bold use of color, dramatic silhouettes, and theatrical designs. Rooted in the Japanese concept of b...

item.title: Rubio Meets Pope Leo to Discuss U.S.-Vatican Relations Amid Tensions over Iran
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with Pope Leo at the Vatican in a private and constructive session, aimed at reinforcing U.S.-Vatican relations following recent tensions between President Dona...

item.title: Examining the Need to Reform Abortion Laws in India
item.description: The recent Supreme Court observation in India regarding a 15-year-old rape survivor seeking termination of a 30-week pregnancy has reignited debates around abortion laws in the country. The court aske...

item.title: Massive Alaska Landslide Causes Record-Breaking Tsunami with Implications for B.C. Coastal Communities
item.description: In August 2025, a landslide in Alaska's Tracy Arm Fjord triggered a colossal tsunami, measuring approximately 481 metres high—higher than the CN Tower’s observation deck. Researchers from the Universi...

item.title: Unique and Unconventional Car Designs from 1900 to 1960
item.description: From the early 1900s through the 1960s, the automotive world was a playground for inventors and visionaries who had little regard for convention. Without strict safety regulations or design norms, eng...

item.title: NDLEA, International Agencies Arrest Nigerian Drug Kingpin in Switzerland
item.description: NDLEA, together with US-DEA and law enforcement from Switzerland, France, and Greece, don catch one Nigerian billionaire wey dem suspect dey run big drug money laundering business across Europe and Na...

item.title: Flatmate disputes over sharing personal details for free trials
item.description: Billy is frustrated with his flatmate Ronnie, who frequently uses Billy’s personal contact details—phone number and email—to register for multiple free trials at gyms and streaming services. Billy obj...

item.title: Proposed foreclosure law may allow banks to repossess mortgaged homes after 90-day default period
item.description: The federal government is considering a legal amendment that would allow commercial banks to take possession of mortgaged homes if borrowers fail to repay their dues within a cumulative 90-day notice ...

item.title: Testosterone and Exercise Reduce Dangerous Visceral Fat in Older Women Recovering from Hip Fractures
item.description: As people age, fat does not just increase; it redistributes in ways that can be harmful, especially in the abdominal area where visceral fat surrounds vital organs. Unlike subcutaneous fat, which is g...

item.title: Israeli air strike kills son of Hamas negotiator amid Gaza peace talks
item.description: An Israeli air strike on Wednesday night resulted in the death of Azzam Al-Hayya, son of Khalil Al-Hayya, Hamas’ chief negotiator in US-mediated talks regarding Gaza's future. This marks the fourth ti...

item.title: Expert Panel Recommends Environmental Clearance for 235-km Varanasi-Kolkata Greenfield Expressway
item.description: New Delhi: In a significant development for infrastructure in eastern India, an expert panel of the Union Environment Ministry has recommended granting environmental clearance to the proposed 235-kilo...

item.title: Hyderabad Police to Auction Over a Thousand Unclaimed Vehicles After Six Months
item.description: The Hyderabad City Police has issued a notification regarding the disposal of 1,130 abandoned and unclaimed vehicles of various types. Citizens who believe they have ownership or legal claims over any...

item.title: Quebec family loses father after 911 calls are mistakenly routed to Ontario
item.description: In Quebec's Abitibi-Témiscamingue region, the Lemire family is grieving the death of Victorin Lemire, who suffered a heart attack at home during a snowstorm on March 16. His son reported that 911 call...

item.title: North Gauteng High Court hears challenge to BBBEE legal sector code
item.description: The North Gauteng High Court in Pretoria is currently hearing a case that challenges the Broad-Based Black Economic Empowerment (BBBEE) legal sector code. This legal code, which governs compliance sta...
